qa_pairs.question: How do I prepare the `table.dat` file for a META-Farm application named `fcode` that needs to read from a series of numbered input files like `data.1`, `data.2`, etc., stored in `/home/user/IC`?
qa_pairs.answer:
To prepare `table.dat` for an application like `fcode` that reads from numbered input files (e.g., `data.X` from `/home/user/IC`), each line in `table.dat` should specify the application and redirect the corresponding input file. For example:
`fcode < /home/user/IC/data.1`
`fcode < /home/user/IC/data.2`
... 
You can use a shell loop to create this file, such as: `for ((i=1; i<=100; i++)); do echo "fcode < /home/user/IC/data.$i"; done >table.dat`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the process for configuring META-Farm when an application requires an input file with a prescribed and unchangeable name (e.g., `INPUT`)?
qa_pairs.answer: If an application expects an input file with a fixed name (e.g., `INPUT`), each case must run in its own subdirectory. You should prepare your distinct input files (e.g., `/path/to/data.X`) and store them separately. Your `table.dat` file will then simply contain the application name repeatedly (e.g., `/path/to/code`). You must also add a line to `single_case.sh` to copy the appropriate input file for each case into its farm subdirectory, renaming it to the prescribed name. For example, add `cp /path/to/data.$ID INPUT` before the `$COMM` line in `single_case.sh`.
